user_prompt: implement `void test_webserver()`, start the Serial port for debug, then use WifiManager to start the default soft AP, then start the web server, then loop forever allowing me to test the web server.
agent_reply: Implemented [test_webserver.cpp](/D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_webserver.cpp).
The test now:
- Starts `Serial`.
- Initializes M5 with mic/speaker off.
- Attempts to initialize the microSD card so the file routes are testable.
- Uses `WifiManager::startGeneratedSoftAp()`.
- Prints the SoftAP SSID, password, and IP address to serial.
- Starts `WebServer::init()`.
- Spins forever for manual testing.
In the current debug bring-up build, the password printed should be:
```text
Verified with:
```powershell
pio run -e thefly_bringuptest -j 4
Build succeeded. The existing SBC and ESP-FTP-Server dependency warnings still appear.

commit_message: starting to add a web server

user_prompt: We have made a lot of progress, a lot of things are working the way I want
Walk through the documentation I wrote earlier, we might've deviated a lot. Warn me where the implementation does not match the written documentation, and tell me suggested changes
Note: I am thinking about making the device way more secure, and the `bluetooth.json` and `wifi.json` will eventually go away, so ignore things that involve that
agent_reply: **Findings**
1. **The documented file-transfer/filesystem design no longer matches the code.**

The docs still say Wi-Fi provides an FTP server, the app treats onboard flash and microSD as separate disks, and LittleFS is used: [README.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/README.md:7), [file-system-and-ftp-server-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/file-system-and-ftp-server-design.md:1).
Current code has:
- no FTP implementation beyond a readme in [lib/FtpServer/readme.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FtpServer/readme.md:1)
- a microSD-only HTTP file server in [WebServer.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebServer.cpp:376)
- that HTTP server is only visibly initialized by the webserver test, not normal firmware startup: [test_webserver.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_webserver.cpp:53)
- only microSD filesystem bring-up in [MicroSdCard.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/MicroSdCard/MicroSdCard.cpp:74)
Suggested change: decide the real transfer direction now.
- If HTTP/web UI is the intended path, update README and filesystem docs from “FTP + LittleFS + two disks” to “microSD file access over HTTP/web UI” and delete or mark FTP notes as obsolete.
- If FTP and onboard flash still matter, they are implementation gaps, not documentation details.
2. **Several documented Wi-Fi and UI workflows are still scaffolding.**

The docs describe AP mode, station selection, NTP sync, cloud upload selection/progress, file viewing, and RTC setting: [ui-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/ui-design.md:3), [wifi-workflow.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/wifi-workflow.md:9).
In the current main flow:
- selecting a configured station, AP, cloud upload, and NTP action only logs: [main.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:530)

- `WifiChooserView`, `WebActionView`, `ApModeView`, and `UploadProgressView` are empty shells: [WifiChooserView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/WifiChooserView.cpp:3), [WebActionView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/WebActionView.cpp:3), [ApModeView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ApModeView.cpp:3), [UploadProgressView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/UploadProgressView.cpp:3)
- `FileListView` is also only a placeholder: [FileListView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/FileListView.cpp:3)
Suggested change: split docs into:
- **implemented now**
- **planned workflow**
That would avoid the docs reading like finished product behavior while these surfaces are still stubs.
3. **The call-recording UI behavior has drifted from the written UI contract.**

The docs say the user can change call volume, mute/unmute the mic, and see a 3-pixel tally border that becomes dotted when local mic is muted: [ui-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/ui-design.md:7). The Bluetooth workflow also says the recording screen shows mute status and output volume: [bluetooth-workflow.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/bluetooth-workflow.md:19).
Current recording UI:
- exposes push-to-talk mode switching and **speaker** mute in [RecordingView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:185)

- uses a fixed 10-pixel solid red frame in [RecordingView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:21) and [RecordingView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:403)
- has mic mute support in `AudioManager`, but I did not find GUI wiring for it: [AudioManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1307)
- has volume APIs, but not a recording-view volume control: [AudioManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1231)
Suggested change: choose one UI model.
- If the device is primarily push-to-talk on internal hardware, rewrite the docs around P2T and speaker mute.
- If mic mute and volume are still required call controls, add those controls and make the tally border encode mic state as documented.
4. **Recorder performance/file-format docs are slightly ahead of current behavior.**

The file-format doc says new recordings should pre-allocate roughly 1 GB and truncate afterward: [recording-file-format.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/recording-file-format.md:7). The dual-core note also assumes pre-grown `.rec` files: [dual-core-load-distribution.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/dual-core-load-distribution.md:113).

Current code has preallocation implemented but disabled by default: [conf.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:14), [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:418).

Also, the doc says packets have a header and payload; the current on-disk implementation writes a **fixed-size** `file_packet_t` for normal `.rec` packets, including a fixed payload array and `payload_length`: [defs.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h:31), [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:230).
Suggested change:
- update the format doc to explicitly say `.rec` packets are fixed-size today
- say preallocation is optional/compile-time unless you intend to enable it again
5. **The high-level audio/hardware docs overstate two areas.**

The high-level design says an external WM8960 or SGTL5000 codec may be enabled by build flag: [high-level-fw-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/high-level-fw-design.md:9). In the current audio manager, external I2S codec enable functions are explicit “not implemented yet” stubs: [AudioManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:626).

The same doc says CVSD local PCM is 8 kHz: [high-level-fw-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/high-level-fw-design.md:31). The implementation accepts 8 kHz HFP, but `AudioFifo` upsamples 8 kHz input into its 16 kHz internal representation, and recorder packets are labeled `*_16KHZ_MONO`: [AudioFifo.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFifo/AudioFifo.h:90), [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:315).
Suggested change:
- state “external codec path planned, internal M5Stack audio path implemented”
- clarify audio-rate layers:
- HFP CVSD ingress/egress can be 8 kHz
- recorder/internal FIFO normalizes to 16 kHz today
**Things that still match well**

- Bluetooth connection auto-starts a call recording after entering a connected/audio-available state, matching the Bluetooth workflow: [bluetooth-workflow.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/bluetooth-workflow.md:13), [main.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:166).

- The recording stop path can also disconnect Bluetooth: [bluetooth-workflow.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/bluetooth-workflow.md:17), [RecordingViewCallbacks.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingViewCallbacks.cpp:64).

- Wi-Fi activation shuts down Bluetooth/audio/recording first, which matches the high-level design: [high-level-fw-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/high-level-fw-design.md:17), [WifiManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp:291).

- RTC-based recording naming and `.rec` output are in place: [high-level-fw-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/high-level-fw-design.md:21), [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:68).
- Top bar date/time and battery status are implemented: [ui-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/ui-design.md:16), [FlyGui.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:630).
**Security Note**

Ignoring the eventual removal of `bluetooth.json` and `wifi.json` as requested: the recording format already has security hooks, but they are placeholders right now. `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY` adds nonce fields and leaves encryption/authentication as TODOs: [defs.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h:39), [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:206).
Before hardening transfer/config storage, I would write one short security design doc that states:
- what is encrypted at rest
- what authenticates a recording packet/file
- how keys are provisioned and recovered
- what local UI actions are allowed without trust/authentication
- whether the HTTP file surface survives at all
I did not count `bluetooth.json` or `wifi.json` mismatches in the findings. I also did not run builds or tests for this pass; this was a documentation-to-code review.

user_prompt: Inside `lib\FtpServer` implement a `lib\FtpServer\FtpServer.cpp` and `lib\FtpServer\FtpServer.h` that's simply a wrapper so that the `kastenklicker/ESP-FTP-Server` library is serving the microSD card. Wire it into `void test_webserver()`, put some comments on how to make it secure, like setting a username and password and if it is using sftp or not.
agent_reply: Implemented.
The new wrapper is in [FtpServer.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FtpServer/FtpServer.h:1) and [FtpServer.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FtpServer/FtpServer.cpp:1). It:
- mounts one Arduino `FS` filesystem as `microSD`
- requires a username and password
- exposes `FtpServer::start()`, `FtpServer::poll()`, and `FtpServer::started()`
- documents that this is plain FTP, not SFTP or FTPS, so credentials do not encrypt the transport: [FtpServer.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FtpServer/FtpServer.cpp:29)
I wired it into [test_webserver.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_webserver.cpp:1):
- starts FTP after the SoftAP and HTTP server
- mounts the card through Arduino `SD` for the FTP library
- services FTP from the test loop every 10 ms
- adds explicit comments for the future security work and the `SdFat` vs Arduino `SD` wrinkle: [test_webserver.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_webserver.cpp:64)

One important caveat is left in the code on purpose: `kastenklicker/ESP-FTP-Server` 0.14.2 takes Arduino `FS`, while the main firmware microSD path uses `SdFat::SdFs`. In this bring-up test, HTTP is still using `MicroSdCard`/SdFat and FTP mounts the same card through Arduino `SD`. That is fine for proving the wrapper, but I would not keep two writable filesystem stacks pointed at the same card in the real Wi-Fi path.
Verified with:
```powershell
pio run -e thefly_bringuptest
Build passed. The build still emits existing warnings from `lib/sbc`, plus a dependency warning that `ESP-FTP-Server` calls deprecated `NetworkServer::available()`.

There are unrelated worktree changes still present in [web_assets.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/web_assets.h) and [package_web_assets.py](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/tools/package_web_assets.py); I left those alone.

user_prompt: I went through `lib\ESP-FTP-Server-Lib` and `src\tests\test_webserver.cpp` to swap out `FS` with `SdFs` and also `File` with `FsFile`
Go through and correct all the mistakes. Do not build
agent_reply: I cleaned up the `SdFs` / `FsFile` conversion mistakes without building.

The main fixes are in [FTPFilesystem.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPFilesystem.h:8) and [FTPFilesystem.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPFilesystem.cpp:22):
- Switched `open()` from Arduino string modes to SdFat `oflag_t` flags with `O_RDONLY`.
- Removed the old virtual-root implementation based on Arduino `fs::FileImpl` and `File`, since that cannot produce an `FsFile`.
- Fixed the bad `fs::SdFs` namespace leftover.
- Made the single mounted `SdFs` act as the FTP root, while still tolerating paths prefixed with the mount name.
I fixed the command handlers’ `FsFile` API assumptions too:
- Added a shared `FsFile::getName(...)` helper in [FTPCommand.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPCommand.h:95).

- Replaced `File::name()` usage in [LIST.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/LIST.h:27), [NLST.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/NLST.h:27), [STAT.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/STAT.h:23), and [MLSD.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/MLSD.h:64).
- Replaced `getLastWrite()` in [MLSD.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/MLSD.h:49) with SdFat modification date/time fields.
- Replaced `"w"` in [STOR.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/STOR.h:24) with `O_WRONLY | O_CREAT | O_TRUNC`, so uploads create or overwrite as FTP `STOR` expects.
I also removed the unused chip-select constant from [test_webserver.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_webserver.cpp:12).

One behavior note: because the Arduino virtual mount-root no longer fits `FsFile`, the current single microSD mount is exposed directly at FTP `/` instead of showing a synthetic `/microSD` directory first. That matches the current wrapper use case, which serves only the microSD card. I did not build, per your instruction.
I cleaned up the `SdFs` / `FsFile` conversion mistakes without building.

The main fixes are in [FTPFilesystem.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPFilesystem.h:8) and [FTPFilesystem.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPFilesystem.cpp:22):
- Switched `open()` from Arduino string modes to SdFat `oflag_t` flags with `O_RDONLY`.
- Removed the old virtual-root implementation based on Arduino `fs::FileImpl` and `File`, since that cannot produce an `FsFile`.
- Fixed the bad `fs::SdFs` namespace leftover.
- Made the single mounted `SdFs` act as the FTP root, while still tolerating paths prefixed with the mount name.
I fixed the command handlers’ `FsFile` API assumptions too:
- Added a shared `FsFile::getName(...)` helper in [FTPCommand.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPCommand.h:95).

- Replaced `File::name()` usage in [LIST.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/LIST.h:27), [NLST.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/NLST.h:27), [STAT.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/STAT.h:23), and [MLSD.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/MLSD.h:64).
- Replaced `getLastWrite()` in [MLSD.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/MLSD.h:49) with SdFat modification date/time fields.
- Replaced `"w"` in [STOR.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/STOR.h:24) with `O_WRONLY | O_CREAT | O_TRUNC`, so uploads create or overwrite as FTP `STOR` expects.
I also removed the unused chip-select constant from [test_webserver.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_webserver.cpp:12).

One behavior note: because the Arduino virtual mount-root no longer fits `FsFile`, the current single microSD mount is exposed directly at FTP `/` instead of showing a synthetic `/microSD` directory first. That matches the current wrapper use case, which serves only the microSD card. I did not build, per your instruction.

user_prompt: look for `#ifdef BUILD_FTP_SERVER` and execute TODOs inside, any added code about the FTP server lives inside `#ifdef BUILD_FTP_SERVER` please
agent_reply: Done. I replaced the guarded FTP TODOs in the runtime path:
- [WebServer.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebServer.cpp:412) starts FTP from `WebServer::init()` under `#ifdef BUILD_FTP_SERVER`.
- [WifiManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp:799) polls FTP under `#ifdef BUILD_FTP_SERVER`.

I put the FTP credentials and security note inside the guarded block in [WebServer.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebServer.cpp:21). The note calls out that this is plain FTP, not SFTP, and the placeholder credentials need replacing before exposure.
I also cleaned up [test_webserver.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_webserver.cpp:14):
- removed the test-local FTP startup/polling path
- made its idle loop poll the `WifiManager` instance, so the normal guarded runtime path drives FTP

One important detail: `WifiManager::poll()` has early returns in the connected/scan paths, so I placed the FTP poll at the top of the method. Leaving it where the TODO was would have skipped FTP servicing during your SoftAP webserver test.
Verification:
- `pio run -e thefly_bringuptest` succeeded.

- That build does **not** compile the guarded FTP additions right now because `BUILD_FTP_SERVER` is not currently defined anywhere in the repo. When you want FTP active, add `-DBUILD_FTP_SERVER` to the environment that should include it.
Done. I replaced the guarded FTP TODOs in the runtime path:
- [WebServer.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebServer.cpp:412) starts FTP from `WebServer::init()` under `#ifdef BUILD_FTP_SERVER`.
- [WifiManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp:799) polls FTP under `#ifdef BUILD_FTP_SERVER`.

I put the FTP credentials and security note inside the guarded block in [WebServer.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebServer.cpp:21). The note calls out that this is plain FTP, not SFTP, and the placeholder credentials need replacing before exposure.
I also cleaned up [test_webserver.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_webserver.cpp:14):
- removed the test-local FTP startup/polling path
- made its idle loop poll the `WifiManager` instance, so the normal guarded runtime path drives FTP

One important detail: `WifiManager::poll()` has early returns in the connected/scan paths, so I placed the FTP poll at the top of the method. Leaving it where the TODO was would have skipped FTP servicing during your SoftAP webserver test.
Verification:
- `pio run -e thefly_bringuptest` succeeded.

- That build does **not** compile the guarded FTP additions right now because `BUILD_FTP_SERVER` is not currently defined anywhere in the repo. When you want FTP active, add `-DBUILD_FTP_SERVER` to the environment that should include it.

user_prompt: the "/get_info" handler needs to include a boolean indicating if the default soft AP is being used or not, as this is what allows the password to be reset
the action of reset is not implemented yet, the handler for that action will also have a check, so having this in the front end is not a problem
use this boolean to show or hide `#password_reset_entry`, and change the `#password_reset_help` to say that a password reset requires the usage of the default soft AP mode
agent_reply: Implemented.
`/get_info` now includes:
```json
"default_soft_ap": true/false
That comes from a new `WifiManager::isGeneratedSoftApActive()` helper, so it is based on the actual generated/default AP instance being active.
Frontend changes:
- `#password_reset_entry` starts hidden.
- `body_onload()` / `render_info()` uses `info.default_soft_ap` to show or hide it.
- `#password_reset_help` now says password reset requires using the device’s default soft AP mode.
I also updated `test_webserver()` so its local `WifiManager` is assigned to the global pointer the web server uses, otherwise this flag would be false during that test.
Regenerated `inc/web_assets.h`.
Verified with:
```powershell
pio run -e thefly_bringuptest
Build passed. Restored the generated `src/version.c` version string afterward.

user_prompt: edit `lib\WifiManager\WifiManager.cpp`, I don't like it being a class object, turn it into a single instance namespace module.
there's a lot of code that looks like `(!wifi_manager)` that is pointless, as if it could be missing, allocating it is not a good idea, it can be totally avoided.
so I want any code that currently looks like `wifi_manager->` to look more like `WifiManager::`
the behavior needs to stay the same, that shouldn't be hard, we never had more than one instance of this thing anyways
It would make the recovery-mode Wi-Fi launch much more easy to think about
agent_reply: Done. `WifiManager` is now a single-instance namespace module instead of an allocated class object.
What changed:

- Converted [WifiManager.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.h) / [WifiManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp) from class state to namespace API + file-scope module state.
- Removed the global `wifi_manager` pointer, heap allocation, `extern` declarations, and pointless `if (!wifi_manager)` branches.
- Updated call sites to use `WifiManager::...`.
- Updated `ScrollView`, `NtpSync`, web handlers, Wi-Fi views, error recovery AP launch, and the affected tests.
- Preserved the old constructor side effect by calling `WifiManager::clear()` during boot and in standalone AP tests.
Verified:
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0`
- `pio run -e thefly_sec1`
- `pio run -e thefly_sec2`
- `git diff --check`
All passed. PlatformIO touched `src/version.c` during builds; I left it as-is.
